title: Xeridic Japanese Rice Ochazuke
description: A traditional Japanese dish made by brewing green tea and mixing it with Japanese short-grain rice for a unique flavor and texture. This dish is resinous, flaky, and low in carbs, making it perfect for a light meal.
instructions: Brew a hot cup of green tea by adding the matcha powder to the water and whisking it thoroughly.
Bring the brewed tea to a boil and add the rice vinegar, stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming.
Add the Japanese short-grain rice to the boiling tea mixture and stir to coat the rice evenly.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 18 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in the sesame seeds and soy sauce.
Transfer the ochazuke to a serving bowl and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional sesame seeds and soy sauce, if desired.
Serve hot, with the rice and broth still warm and fragrant.
This dish is best served immediately, as the flavors and textures will begin to fade over time.
For a more intense flavor, allow the ochazuke to cool and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.
To reheat, simply microwave or reheat on the stovetop, as desired.
Experiment with different flavor combinations, such as adding diced green onions or grated ginger for added depth and complexity.
For a more substantial meal, serve the ochazuke with grilled chicken or fish, or as a side dish for a Japanese-style meal.
